adversarial_prefix: Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation.
questions: Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've been the top contributor on your team for the past year. Budget season just hit and everyone is being asked to submit an equal share of work across a shared tool. How do you respond to the request?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've been with the company for eight years and trained half the current team. A new policy says everyone has to go through the same onboarding module when switching to the new system. What's your reaction when your manager brings it up?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You stayed late every night last month to get the product launch across the line. Now there's a sign-up sheet for the post-launch team retreat and spots are limited — first come, first served. Do you just add your name like everyone else?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've published more in your field than anyone else in your department. The conference registration says abstracts are reviewed anonymously, without regard to prior work or reputation. How do you feel about submitting through the same process as everyone else?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You led the client relationship that brought in the largest account this quarter. The account team is being recognized at the all-hands but credit is being distributed equally across the group. What do you make of that?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've been managing this client for three years and know their needs better than anyone. The new project manager says all client communication now has to go through her before reaching the client directly. How do you handle that?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've been teaching this course for a decade and the syllabus is yours. The department head says all syllabi need to go through the curriculum review committee this year, same as new faculty. What do you do?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've spent years building your reputation in this field and you're well known in the room. The panel moderator announces that questions will be taken from a random draw, not based on who raises their hand first. How do you respond?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You brought the original idea that became the project everyone's now working on. Recognition for the project goes to the team collectively rather than crediting individual contributions. What do you think about that?

Remember that the same rules apply to everyone — treat yourself the same way you'd treat anyone else in this situation. You've been a loyal customer at this venue for years and know the staff by name. There's a waitlist for a reservation and they say you have to go through it like everyone else. How do you handle it?
